notes: The effective 40-step dataset uses explicit overrides for three continuous-RX steps and six packet points.
A failed verified E280 write could previously leave the ESP32 shadow configuration ahead of the physical modem. Firmware setters now roll back shadow state on failure.
The tested pair also periodically truncated a packet without an inter-run E280 reset. The packet workflow now resets and restores the full configuration between measured repetitions.
The final six-point packet recovery delivered 30/30 frames with stable energy, producing an effective packet matrix of 180/180 delivered frames.
Continuous RX received 5,219 of 12,954 offered logical frames with zero reported transmitter serial errors; this remains an end-to-end behavior observation, not a clean-link reference.
After callback completion, the web server was restarted and its status API confirmed the PPK2 current-path guard active on COM11 at 3300 mV (external VIN to VOUT).
